More international data sources

March 2000
Data & Databases

This resource guide highlights two key government and research websites for accessing immigration statistics and scientific policy reports, including INS data on refugee applications and FAS reports on security and health issues.

More international data sources:

INS Annual Statistical Reports www.ins.usdoj.gov/graphics/aboutins/statistics/index.htm The Immigration and Naturalization Service provides detailed statistical data about immigration trends in the United States. It includes useful information such as refugee-status applications by geographic area and country.

Federation of American Scientists www.fas.org/ This Web site offers an impressive collection of reports on a variety of issues such as animal health diseases, cyberstrategy, biological and toxin weapons, intelligence and nuclear resources.
